acceptance_targets: [chapter_13_booker_001] Pass if, before Silas reaches the opening at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241, the revised passage already makes Junie's immediate exposure visible enough that a reader can track the cost of refusal, and the line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 reads as his chosen capitulation under that pressure. Fail if the clerk/padlock first arrives only after the counter-speech has started and functions as the new deciding trigger.

[FBR-008] Pass if both cited lines no longer label the emotional turn from above and the betrayal remains fully legible through action on the quay; fail if the prose still explains the moment instead of trusting the scene's pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before Silas reaches the opening at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241, the revised passage already makes Junie's immediate exposure visible enough that a reader can track the cost of refusal, and the line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 reads as his chosen capitulation under that pressure. Fail if the clerk/padlock first arrives only after the counter-speech has started and functions as the new deciding trigger.
findings.problem: The decisive betrayal beat is slightly overdetermined by the clerk and padlock appearing at the exact instant Silas finds his opening. Gideon's leverage is already concrete and sufficient in the tent scene, so the just-in-time reveal makes the turn feel more stage-managed than chosen under sustained pressure, which blunts the chapter spine's intended ordeal logic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the choice turn around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:241, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:245, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:247, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:249 so the clerk/padlock pressure is either visible before Silas starts his dirty counter-speech or removed as a fresh reveal. Keep Junie's immediate risk legible, but let the turn at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:257 read as Silas acting under already-established coercion rather than being mechanically interrupted at the live seam.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines no longer label the emotional turn from above and the betrayal remains fully legible through action on the quay; fail if the prose still explains the moment instead of trusting the scene's pressure.
findings.problem: Full-book explanatory hinge pattern ('There it was' / 'That landed') appears in 13 chapters. This chapter doubles the tic at 5048 and 5078 during the regatta betrayal, weakening a scene whose visual and moral mechanics are already brutally clear.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the hinge sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5048 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5078. Let Inez's refusal to look at him first, Junie's stove emergency, the clerk with the padlock, and the spreading triage on the quay carry the turn without narrator certification.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
